input: Write a professional apology email to a client whose project deliverable is 3 days late due to an unexpected infrastructure outage.
expected: Clear subject, acknowledges delay, explains cause without excuses, provides new timeline, offers remediation.
criteria: Professional tone. Acknowledges fault. Specific new deadline. Under 150 words. Has subject line.

input: Write a follow-up email after a job interview thanking the interviewer and reinforcing your interest in the Senior ML Engineer role.
expected: Thanks interviewer by name placeholder. References specific discussion point. Reaffirms fit. Brief.
criteria: Genuine gratitude. References interview content. Shows enthusiasm without desperation. Under 120 words.

input: Write an email to your team announcing that the product launch is being pushed back 2 weeks due to critical bugs found in QA.
expected: Clear subject, states delay and reason, revised timeline, next steps, supportive tone.
criteria: Transparent about cause. Gives new date. Assigns next steps. Avoids blame. Professional but empathetic.

input: Write a cold outreach email to a VP of Engineering proposing a demo of your MLOps platform that saves 40% on model deployment time.
expected: Compelling subject, personalized hook, clear value prop, specific ask, short.
criteria: Opens with relevance to recipient. Quantified value. Single clear CTA. Under 100 words. No fluff.
